title: Bounded XML Decoding, Encodings, Entities, and Structural Security · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds strict bounded XML admission boundary from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of strict bounded XML admission boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Enforce compressed and decoded byte, token, element-depth, attribute, text, item, and duration limits before allocation grows unbounded.
Keep encoding/xml strict mode, inspect tokens, reject malformed nesting and trailing documents, and never install arbitrary entity maps.
Use an allowlisted CharsetReader, reconcile transport and XML declarations, and reject unsupported or conflicting encodings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Byte Depth Budgets
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 6045 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit bounded strict XML while rejecting malformed, deep, entity, encoding, and trailing cases; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce compressed and decoded byte, token, element-depth, attribute, text, item, and duration limits before allocation grows unbounded.
